readout_role_assumption: CPMG.xml reviewed before launch and terminal metadata verifies CPMG.xml; readout1 true-0 reference, readout2 pi/ms1 reference, readout3 final CPMG echo candidate signal.

drift_summary.reference_method: scan-order-aware average drift diagnostic over terminal 12-average savedexperiment

interpretation: Terminal CPMG shows a readout3/final-echo dip in the 1/(4 f13) target region rather than exactly at the nearest precomputed grid point. The minimum is at tau=0.6736 us (+23.6 ns from the 0.6500 us model target, within the modeled ~81 ns filter-width scale), with readout3/self=0.9528 and similar reference-normalized values (0.9518 over readout1 baseline, 0.9568 over readout2 baseline). The same point is present in the even-subset trend after 6, 8, 10, and 12 averages. This is not a normalization-only artifact and it corroborates the earlier weak det-shift Ramsey candidate. The alternate 1/(2f13) convention has only percent-scale/weak support. Supported conclusion: reimage1804_c02 likely has a nearby 13C-like coupling signature; strength is moderate/weak, with no precise coupling extraction from this scan alone. Bridge terminal status remains clean: 12 averages x 50000 reps, CPMG.xml, snake scan order, healthy pre/post counts, and no drift flags.
